```{r permutation_test_setup}
# =============================================================================
# PERMUTATION TEST FOR MODEL DISCRIMINATION
# =============================================================================
# Tests whether the best model's AUROC significantly exceeds chance by shuffling
# outcome labels through the FULL nested CV pipeline.
#
# Target model: rf_base | Approach3_DataDriven | Full_Microbiome
# Observed AUROC: 0.813 ± 0.110 post-decontam
#
# CRITICAL DESIGN DECISIONS:
#   1. Labels shuffled at SUBJECT level (not sample level) to preserve
#      longitudinal correlation structure
#   2. Shuffled labels propagated to BOTH clinical_data_all AND subject_labels
#      (subject_labels is used for inner split stratification)
#   3. Same cv_folds object used (fold membership fixed, only labels change)
#   4. Full pipeline re-run each permutation: feature selection, CLR, threshold
#      optimization, model training — no shortcuts
#
# RUNTIME ESTIMATE:
#   ~3-5 min per permutation (single core)
#   500 permutations: ~25-40 hours sequential
#   With 8 cores: ~3-5 hours
#   Recommendation: test with n_perm=10 first, then scale up
# =============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(ranger)
library(glmnet)
library(pROC)
library(PRROC)
library(parallel)
library(doParallel)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CONFIGURATION
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

n_permutations <- 572
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1  # Leave 1 core free for system
# Adjust n_cores based on your machine's RAM: each worker needs ~2GB

# Observed AUROC from best model 
observed_auroc <- 0.813  # rf_base | Approach3_DataDriven | Full_Microbiome

# Output directory
output_dir <- "decontam_results"  # Change if needed
if(!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

cat(sprintf("Permutation test configuration:\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Permutations: %d\n", n_permutations))
cat(sprintf("  Cores available: %d (using %d)\n", 
            parallel::detectCores(), n_cores))
cat(sprintf("  Observed AUROC: %.3f\n", observed_auroc))
cat(sprintf("  Output directory: %s\n\n", output_dir))
```

```{r permutation_shuffle_function}
# =============================================================================
# FUNCTION: Shuffle labels at subject level and propagate to all data objects
# =============================================================================

shuffle_preterm_labels <- function(clinical_data_all, subject_labels, seed) {
  # Shuffles preterm labels at the SUBJECT level and applies them consistently
  # to both clinical_data_all and subject_labels.
  #
  # Args:
  #   clinical_data_all: full clinical dataset (sample-level, has preterm column)
  #   subject_labels:    subject-level labels (used for inner split stratification)
  #   seed:              random seed for this permutation
  #
  # Returns:
  #   list with $clinical_data_perm and $subject_labels_perm
  
  set.seed(seed)
  
  # Step 1: Get unique subject-outcome mapping
  subject_outcomes <- subject_labels %>%
    select(id, preterm)
  
  # Step 2: Shuffle preterm labels across subjects
  # This preserves the marginal distribution (same number of PTB/term)
  shuffled_labels <- subject_outcomes %>%
    mutate(preterm_shuffled = sample(preterm))
  
  # Step 3: Apply shuffled labels to clinical_data_all (sample-level)
  # Each sample inherits the shuffled label of its subject
  clinical_data_perm <- clinical_data_all %>%
    select(-preterm) %>%
    left_join(
      shuffled_labels %>% select(id, preterm = preterm_shuffled),
      by = "id"
    )
  
  # Step 4: Update subject_labels (used for inner split stratification)
  subject_labels_perm <- shuffled_labels %>%
    select(id, preterm = preterm_shuffled)
  
  return(list(
    clinical_data_perm = clinical_data_perm,
    subject_labels_perm = subject_labels_perm
  ))
}
```

```{r permutation_single_run}
# =============================================================================
# FUNCTION: Run ONE permutation (full nested CV pipeline)
# =============================================================================

run_single_permutation <- function(perm_idx,
                                    clinical_data_all,
                                    microbiome_data_all,
                                    subject_labels,
                                    cv_folds,
                                    model_spec,
                                    approach_name = "Approach3_DataDriven",
                                    microbiome_option = "Full_Microbiome") {
  # Runs the full nested CV pipeline with permuted labels.
  # Returns mean AUROC across folds for this permutation.
  #
  # This function is designed to be called in parallel.
  # It suppresses verbose output from train_with_nested_cv.
  
  # Generate unique seed for this permutation
  perm_seed <- 10000 + perm_idx
  
  # Shuffle labels
  shuffled <- shuffle_preterm_labels(clinical_data_all, subject_labels, perm_seed)
  
  # CRITICAL: Temporarily replace subject_labels in the function's environment
  # We need train_with_nested_cv to use shuffled subject_labels for inner splits.
  # Two options:
  #   (a) Modify train_with_nested_cv to accept subject_labels as argument
  #   (b) Assign to the global/parent environment before calling
  #
  # Option (a) is cleaner but requires modifying the function.
  # Option (b) works with the existing function.
  # We use option (b) here — assign to parent env, restore after.
  
  # Save originals
  original_subject_labels <- subject_labels
  
  # Overwrite in the environment where train_with_nested_cv looks for it
  assign("subject_labels", shuffled$subject_labels_perm, envir = parent.frame())
  
  # Run the full pipeline (suppress output)
  result <- tryCatch({
    invisible(capture.output({
      res <- train_with_nested_cv(
        model_name = "rf_base",
        model_spec = model_spec,
        clinical_data_all = shuffled$clinical_data_perm,
        microbiome_data_all = microbiome_data_all,
        approach_name = approach_name,
        microbiome_option = microbiome_option,
        cv_folds = cv_folds,
        use_pca = FALSE
      )
    }))
    
    if(!is.null(res) && !is.null(res$summary)) {
      res$summary$AUROC_mean
    } else {
      NA_real_
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning(sprintf("Permutation %d failed: %s", perm_idx, e$message))
    NA_real_
  })
  
  # Restore originals
  assign("subject_labels", original_subject_labels, envir = parent.frame())
  
  return(result)
}
```

```{r permutation_parallel_wrapper}
# =============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVE: Self-contained permutation function for parallel execution
# =============================================================================
# When using parallel::parLapply or future, each worker needs ALL objects
# and functions in its environment. This wrapper is self-contained.
# =============================================================================

run_permutation_standalone <- function(perm_idx,
                                        clinical_data_all,
                                        microbiome_data_all,
                                        subject_labels_original,
                                        cv_folds,
                                        model_spec) {
  # Self-contained function for parallel execution.
  # Each worker gets all necessary data and functions.
  
  perm_seed <- 10000 + perm_idx
  set.seed(perm_seed)
  
  # ── Step 1: Shuffle labels at subject level ──
  shuffled_map <- subject_labels_original %>%
    mutate(preterm_shuffled = sample(preterm))
  
  # Apply to clinical data
  clinical_perm <- clinical_data_all %>%
    select(-preterm) %>%
    left_join(
      shuffled_map %>% select(id, preterm = preterm_shuffled),
      by = "id"
    )
  
  # Create permuted subject_labels for inner splits
  subject_labels_perm <- shuffled_map %>%
    select(id, preterm = preterm_shuffled)
  
  # ── Step 2: Assign permuted subject_labels to global env ──
  # train_with_nested_cv accesses subject_labels from its parent environment
  # In parallel workers, we need to ensure it's available
  assign("subject_labels", subject_labels_perm, envir = globalenv())
  
  # ── Step 3: Run full pipeline ──
  result_auroc <- tryCatch({
    invisible(capture.output({
      res <- train_with_nested_cv(
        model_name = "rf_base",
        model_spec = model_spec,
        clinical_data_all = clinical_perm,
        microbiome_data_all = microbiome_data_all,
        approach_name = "Approach3_DataDriven",
        microbiome_option = "Full_Microbiome",
        cv_folds = cv_folds,
        use_pca = FALSE
      )
    }))
    
    if(!is.null(res) && !is.null(res$summary)) {
      res$summary$AUROC_mean
    } else {
      NA_real_
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    NA_real_
  })
  
  # ── Step 4: Restore original subject_labels ──
  assign("subject_labels", subject_labels_original, envir = globalenv())
  
  return(result_auroc)
}
```

```{r run_permutation_test_sequential}
# =============================================================================
# OPTION A: SEQUENTIAL EXECUTION (simpler, for testing or small n_perm)
# =============================================================================
# Use this for initial testing with n_perm = 10-20
# Switch to parallel (Option B) for n_perm = 500
# =============================================================================

run_permutation_test_sequential <- function(n_perm,
                                             clinical_data_all,
                                             microbiome_data_all,
                                             subject_labels,
                                             cv_folds,
                                             model_spec) {
  
  cat(sprintf("\n╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n"))
  cat(sprintf("║  PERMUTATION TEST (SEQUENTIAL): %d permutations    ║\n", n_perm))
  cat(sprintf("╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n"))
  
  # Verify data dimensions
  cat(sprintf("Subjects: %d (PTB: %d, Term: %d)\n",
              nrow(subject_labels),
              sum(subject_labels$preterm == "1"),
              sum(subject_labels$preterm == "0")))
  cat(sprintf("Samples: %d\n", nrow(clinical_data_all)))
  
  null_aurocs <- numeric(n_perm)
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  
  for(perm in 1:n_perm) {
    
    perm_start <- Sys.time()
    
    null_aurocs[perm] <- run_permutation_standalone(
      perm_idx = perm,
      clinical_data_all = clinical_data_all,
      microbiome_data_all = microbiome_data_all,
      subject_labels_original = subject_labels,
      cv_folds = cv_folds,
      model_spec = model_spec
    )
    
    perm_elapsed <- as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), perm_start, units = "mins"))
    total_elapsed <- as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"))
    avg_per_perm <- total_elapsed / perm
    est_remaining <- avg_per_perm * (n_perm - perm)
    
    if(perm %% 10 == 0 || perm <= 5) {
      cat(sprintf("  [%3d/%d] AUROC=%.3f | This: %.1f min | Avg: %.1f min | ETA: %.1f min (%.1f h)\n",
                  perm, n_perm,
                  null_aurocs[perm],
                  perm_elapsed,
                  avg_per_perm,
                  est_remaining,
                  est_remaining / 60))
    }
  }
  
  total_time <- as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"))
  cat(sprintf("\nCompleted in %.1f minutes (%.1f hours)\n", total_time, total_time / 60))
  
  return(null_aurocs)
}
```

```{r run_permutation_test_parallel}
# =============================================================================
# OPTION B: PARALLEL EXECUTION (recommended for n_perm = 500)
# =============================================================================

run_permutation_test_parallel <- function(n_perm,
                                           n_cores,
                                           clinical_data_all,
                                           microbiome_data_all,
                                           subject_labels,
                                           cv_folds,
                                           model_spec) {
  
  cat(sprintf("\n╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n"))
  cat(sprintf("║  PERMUTATION TEST (PARALLEL): %d permutations      ║\n", n_perm))
  cat(sprintf("║  Using %d cores                                     ║\n", n_cores))
  cat(sprintf("╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n"))
  
  cat(sprintf("Subjects: %d (PTB: %d, Term: %d)\n",
              nrow(subject_labels),
              sum(subject_labels$preterm == "1"),
              sum(subject_labels$preterm == "0")))
  cat(sprintf("Samples: %d\n\n", nrow(clinical_data_all)))
  
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  
  # ── Create cluster ──
  cl <- makeCluster(n_cores)
  
  # ── Export ALL required objects to workers ──
  # This includes: data, functions, and loaded packages
  clusterExport(cl, varlist = c(
    # Data objects
    "clinical_data_all",
    "microbiome_data_all", 
    "subject_labels",
    "cv_folds",
    "model_spec",
    # Functions from the main workflow
    "train_with_nested_cv",
    "detect_prob_col",
    "apply_clr_transform",
    "optimize_threshold_cv",
    "run_ancombc_on_fold",
    "calculate_univariate_association",
    "run_permutation_standalone",
    # Any other objects train_with_nested_cv references from global env
    "micro_genus_full"  # If referenced; remove if not needed
  ), envir = environment())
  
  # ── Load required packages on each worker ──
  clusterEvalQ(cl, {
    library(tidyverse)
    library(tidymodels)
    library(ranger)
    library(glmnet)
    library(pROC)
    library(PRROC)
  })
  
  cat("Cluster initialized. Starting permutations...\n\n")
  
  # ── Run permutations in parallel ──
  # Process in batches for progress reporting
  batch_size <- min(50, n_perm)
  n_batches <- ceiling(n_perm / batch_size)
  null_aurocs <- numeric(n_perm)
  
  for(batch in 1:n_batches) {
    batch_start_idx <- (batch - 1) * batch_size + 1
    batch_end_idx <- min(batch * batch_size, n_perm)
    batch_indices <- batch_start_idx:batch_end_idx
    
    batch_results <- parLapply(cl, batch_indices, function(perm_idx) {
      run_permutation_standalone(
        perm_idx = perm_idx,
        clinical_data_all = clinical_data_all,
        microbiome_data_all = microbiome_data_all,
        subject_labels_original = subject_labels,
        cv_folds = cv_folds,
        model_spec = model_spec
      )
    })
    
    null_aurocs[batch_indices] <- unlist(batch_results)
    
    elapsed <- as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"))
    completed <- batch_end_idx
    avg_per_perm <- elapsed / completed
    est_remaining <- avg_per_perm * (n_perm - completed)
    
    cat(sprintf("  Batch %d/%d complete [%d/%d perms] | Elapsed: %.1f min | ETA: %.1f min (%.1f h)\n",
                batch, n_batches, completed, n_perm,
                elapsed, est_remaining, est_remaining / 60))
    
    # Save intermediate results (crash protection)
    saveRDS(
      list(
        null_aurocs = null_aurocs[1:completed],
        n_completed = completed,
        n_total = n_perm,
        elapsed_min = elapsed
      ),
      file.path(output_dir, "permutation_checkpoint.rds")
    )
  }
  
  # ── Cleanup ──
  stopCluster(cl)
  
  total_time <- as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"))
  cat(sprintf("\nCompleted %d permutations in %.1f minutes (%.1f hours)\n",
              n_perm, total_time, total_time / 60))
  
  return(null_aurocs)
}
```

```{r execute_permutation_test}
# =============================================================================
# EXECUTE THE PERMUTATION TEST
# =============================================================================
# STEP 1: Quick test run (10 permutations, sequential)
# STEP 2: Full run (500 permutations, parallel)
# =============================================================================

# ── STEP 1: Test run (UNCOMMENT TO RUN) ──
cat("=== TEST RUN: 10 permutations (sequential) ===\n")
test_aurocs <- run_permutation_test_sequential(
 n_perm = 10,
 clinical_data_all = approach3_clinical_all,  # Approach 3 clinical data
 microbiome_data_all = micro_genus_full,       # Full microbiome
 subject_labels = subject_labels,              # Subject-level labels
 cv_folds = cv_folds,                          # Same CV folds as primary analysis
 model_spec = rf_spec                          # Random Forest spec
)
cat(sprintf("\nTest null AUROCs: %s\n", 
           paste(round(test_aurocs, 3), collapse = ", ")))
cat(sprintf("Test null mean: %.3f ± %.3f\n", mean(test_aurocs), sd(test_aurocs)))

# ── STEP 2: Full run (UNCOMMENT TO RUN) ──
null_aurocs <- run_permutation_test_parallel(
  n_perm = n_permutations,
  n_cores = n_cores,
  clinical_data_all = approach3_clinical_all,  # Approach 3 clinical data
  microbiome_data_all = micro_genus_full,       # Full microbiome
  subject_labels = subject_labels,              # Subject-level labels
  cv_folds = cv_folds,                          # Same CV folds
  model_spec = rf_spec                          # Random Forest spec
)
```

```{r permutation_results}
# =============================================================================
# ANALYZE AND REPORT PERMUTATION TEST RESULTS
# =============================================================================
null_aurocs <- test_aurocs

# Remove any failed permutations (NAs)
valid_aurocs <- null_aurocs[!is.na(null_aurocs)]
n_valid <- length(valid_aurocs)
n_failed <- sum(is.na(null_aurocs))

if(n_failed > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("WARNING: %d/%d permutations failed (%.1f%%)\n",
              n_failed, n_permutations, 100 * n_failed / n_permutations))
  cat("Results based on valid permutations only.\n\n")
}

# ── Calculate permutation p-value ──
# One-sided test: proportion of null AUROCs >= observed
perm_p_value <- (sum(valid_aurocs >= observed_auroc) + 1) / (n_valid + 1)
# +1/+1 correction: conservative estimate (Phipson & Smyth, 2010)

# ── Summary statistics ──
cat("\n╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
cat("║         PERMUTATION TEST RESULTS                   ║\n")
cat("╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")

cat(sprintf("  Model: rf_base | Approach3_DataDriven | Full_Microbiome\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Observed AUROC:      %.3f\n", observed_auroc))
cat(sprintf("  Valid permutations:  %d / %d\n", n_valid, n_permutations))
cat(sprintf("\n  Null distribution:\n"))
cat(sprintf("    Mean ± SD:         %.3f ± %.3f\n", mean(valid_aurocs), sd(valid_aurocs)))
cat(sprintf("    Median:            %.3f\n", median(valid_aurocs)))
cat(sprintf("    Range:             %.3f – %.3f\n", min(valid_aurocs), max(valid_aurocs)))
cat(sprintf("    IQR:               %.3f – %.3f\n",
            quantile(valid_aurocs, 0.25), quantile(valid_aurocs, 0.75)))
cat(sprintf("\n  Permutation p-value: %.4f\n", perm_p_value))
cat(sprintf("    (%d of %d null AUROCs ≥ observed %.3f)\n",
            sum(valid_aurocs >= observed_auroc), n_valid, observed_auroc))

if(perm_p_value < 0.05) {
  cat("\n  ✓ CONCLUSION: Model discrimination significantly exceeds chance (p < 0.05)\n")
} else {
  cat("\n  ✗ CONCLUSION: Model discrimination does NOT significantly exceed chance\n")
}

# ── Save results ──
perm_results_df <- tibble(
  permutation = 1:n_permutations,
  null_auroc = null_aurocs,
  valid = !is.na(null_aurocs)
)

write_csv(perm_results_df, 
          file.path(output_dir, "permutation_null_distribution.csv"))

perm_summary <- tibble(
  observed_auroc = observed_auroc,
  null_mean = mean(valid_aurocs),
  null_sd = sd(valid_aurocs),
  null_median = median(valid_aurocs),
  null_min = min(valid_aurocs),
  null_max = max(valid_aurocs),
  n_permutations = n_permutations,
  n_valid = n_valid,
  n_exceeding = sum(valid_aurocs >= observed_auroc),
  p_value = perm_p_value
)

write_csv(perm_summary,
          file.path(output_dir, "permutation_test_summary.csv"))

cat(sprintf("\nResults saved to:\n"))
cat(sprintf("  %s/permutation_null_distribution.csv\n", output_dir))
cat(sprintf("  %s/permutation_test_summary.csv\n", output_dir))
```

```{r permutation_plot}
# =============================================================================
# VISUALIZATION: Null distribution with observed AUROC
# =============================================================================

library(ggplot2)

# Prepare data
plot_df <- tibble(null_auroc = valid_aurocs)

# Determine sensible x-axis range
x_min <- min(0.3, min(valid_aurocs) - 0.05)
x_max <- max(observed_auroc + 0.05, max(valid_aurocs) + 0.05)

p_perm <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = null_auroc)) +
  # Null distribution
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "gray70", color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
  # Chance line (AUROC = 0.5)
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, color = "gray40", 
             linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  # Observed AUROC
  geom_vline(xintercept = observed_auroc, color = "#D32F2F",
             linewidth = 1.3, linetype = "solid") +
  # Annotations
  annotate("text",
           x = observed_auroc - 0.02, y = Inf,
           label = sprintf("Observed\nAUROC = %.3f", observed_auroc),
           hjust = 1, vjust = 2, color = "#D32F2F", 
           size = 3.8, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text",
           x = 0.5, y = Inf,
           label = "Chance\n(AUROC = 0.5)",
           hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1.2, color = "gray40", 
           size = 3, fontface = "italic") +
  # p-value annotation
  annotate("label",
           x = x_max - 0.02, y = Inf,
           label = sprintf("p = %.4f\n(%d/%d ≥ observed)",
                           perm_p_value,
                           sum(valid_aurocs >= observed_auroc),
                           n_valid),
           hjust = 1, vjust = 2, 
           fill = ifelse(perm_p_value < 0.05, "#C8E6C9", "#FFCDD2"),
           color = "black", size = 3.2, fontface = "bold",
           label.size = 0.5) +
  # Axes and theme
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(x_min, x_max)) +
  labs(
    title = "Permutation Test: Model Discrimination vs. Chance",
    subtitle = sprintf("rf_base | Approach 3 | Full Microbiome | %d permutations with subject-level label shuffling",
                       n_valid),
    x = "Mean AUROC (null distribution)",
    y = "Count",
    caption = sprintf("Null: %.3f ± %.3f | Observed: %.3f | p = %.4f",
                      mean(valid_aurocs), sd(valid_aurocs), 
                      observed_auroc, perm_p_value)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Save
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "permutation_test_histogram.pdf"), p_perm,
       width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "permutation_test_histogram.png"), p_perm,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

cat("Plots saved.\n")
print(p_perm)
```

```{r supplementary_fold_metrics}
# =============================================================================
# SUPPLEMENTARY TABLE: Per-fold metrics for best model
# =============================================================================
# The reviewer also requested: "Per-fold metrics should appear in a
# supplementary table."
# =============================================================================

# Extract fold-level results from the best model
# Assumes cv_results_all is still in memory from primary analysis
best_model_idx <- which(sapply(cv_results_all, function(x) {
  x$model_name == "rf_base" & 
  x$approach == "Approach3_DataDriven" & 
  x$microbiome == "Full_Microbiome"
}))

if(length(best_model_idx) > 0) {
  best_fold_results <- cv_results_all[[best_model_idx[1]]]$fold_results
  
  cat("\n=== SUPPLEMENTARY TABLE: Per-Fold Metrics ===\n\n")
  cat("Model: rf_base | Approach3_DataDriven | Full_Microbiome\n\n")
  
  # Format for display
  fold_table <- best_fold_results %>%
    select(fold, n_test, n_ptb, threshold, AUROC, PRAUC, 
           Sensitivity, Specificity, Balanced_Accuracy, Youden) %>%
    mutate(across(c(threshold, AUROC, PRAUC, Sensitivity, Specificity,
                    Balanced_Accuracy, Youden), ~round(., 3)))
  
  print(fold_table)
  
  # Add summary row
  cat("\n--- Summary (mean ± SD) ---\n")
  summary_row <- fold_table %>%
    summarize(
      across(c(threshold, AUROC, PRAUC, Sensitivity, Specificity,
               Balanced_Accuracy, Youden),
             list(mean = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
                  sd = ~sd(., na.rm = TRUE)))
    )
  
  cat(sprintf("  AUROC:      %.3f ± %.3f\n", summary_row$AUROC_mean, summary_row$AUROC_sd))
  cat(sprintf("  PRAUC:      %.3f ± %.3f\n", summary_row$PRAUC_mean, summary_row$PRAUC_sd))
  cat(sprintf("  Sens:       %.3f ± %.3f\n", summary_row$Sensitivity_mean, summary_row$Sensitivity_sd))
  cat(sprintf("  Spec:       %.3f ± %.3f\n", summary_row$Specificity_mean, summary_row$Specificity_sd))
  cat(sprintf("  Bal Acc:    %.3f ± %.3f\n", summary_row$Balanced_Accuracy_mean, summary_row$Balanced_Accuracy_sd))
  cat(sprintf("  Threshold:  %.3f ± %.3f\n", summary_row$threshold_mean, summary_row$threshold_sd))
  
  # Save as CSV for supplementary material
  write_csv(fold_table, file.path(output_dir, "supplementary_per_fold_metrics.csv"))
  cat(sprintf("\nSaved to: %s/supplementary_per_fold_metrics.csv\n", output_dir))
  
} else {
  cat("WARNING: Best model not found in cv_results_all.\n")
  cat("Run the primary nested CV analysis first.\n")
}
```
